title: Ratepayer Protection Pledge
summary: Proclamation 11014, the Ratepayer Protection Pledge, asserts that the United States' economic and technological leadership depends on domestic data center infrastructure for the internet, cloud computing, and AI. It emphasizes that the expansion of data centers and AI's increasing electricity demand must not raise household energy costs. To achieve this, leading hyperscalers and AI companies have pledged to cover the full cost of energy and infrastructure for their operations. They will secure new generation resources, pay for power delivery infrastructure upgrades, negotiate separate rate structures with utilities and state governments, and invest in local communities and grid reliability. This initiative aims to protect American consumers from higher energy prices, enhance grid resilience, and maintain U.S. global leadership in innovation, with seven major technology companies already accepting the pledge.
